LAW AND MOTION, 302, RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS OVERRULED. RESPONDENT HAS NOT MET ITS BURDEN OF ESTABLISHING THAT PETITIONER DID NOT PLEAD EXHAUSTION OF ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. RESPONDENT HAS NOT MET ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT ANY STATUTE OR REGULATION OR COURT DECISION REQUIRED PETITIONER TO FURTHER PURSUE ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. THE DEMURRER TO THE 2ND AND 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION ARE BASED ESSENTIALLY ON THE SAME GROUNDS AS THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION AND ARE ALSO OVERRULED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/MJM)

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENTS AMEC CONSTRUCTION MANAGEMENT, INC. AND AMEC ENVIRONMENTAL & INFRASTRUCTURE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. THE FIRST AMENDED PETITION DOES NOT PLEAD FACTS ESTABLISHING THAT RESPONDENT AMEC'S DUTY TO PAY THE PREVAILING WAGE ARISES FROM "AN OFFICE, TRUST, OR STATION" (CCP SEC. 1085). A STATUTORY DUTY IS NOT EQUIVALENT TO A MINISTERIAL DUTY. THE BALANCE OF THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: MARLA J. ATTY. ROBERT R. ROGINSON OF OGLETREE, DEAKINS LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (213)239-9800 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT AMEC CONSTRUCTION MANAGEMENT, INC.

RULING - A R G U E D; RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS OVERRULED. RESPONDENT HAS NOT MET ITS BURDEN OF ESTABLISHING THAT PETITIONER DID NOT PLEAD EXHAUSTION OF ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. RESPONDENT HAS NOT MET ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT ANY STATUTE OR REGULATION OR COURT DECISION REQUIRED PETITIONER TO FURTHER PURSUE ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. THE DEMURRER TO THE 2ND AND 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION ARE BASED ESSENTIALLY ON THE SAME GROUNDS AS THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION AND ARE ALSO OVERRULED. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/mjm

ATTY. ALICE L. RAMSEY, DEPUTY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. (916) 654-2630 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT CALTRANS;
ATTY. ROBERT R. ROGINSON OF OGLETREE, DEAKINS LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (213)239-9800 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT AMEC CONSTRUCTION MANAGEMENT, INC.

RULING - A R G U E D; RESPONDENTS AMEC CONSTRUCTION MANAGEMENT, INC. and AMEC ENVIRONMENTAL & INFRASTRUCTURE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. THE FIRST AMENDED PETITION DOES NOT PLEAD FACTS ESTABLISHING THAT RESPONDENT AMEC'S DUTY TO PAY THE PREVAILING WAGE ARISES FROM "AN OFFICE, TRUST, OR STATION" (CCP SEC. 1085). A STATUTORY DUTY IS NOT EQUIVALENT TO A MINISTERIAL DUTY. THE BALANCE OF THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/MJM)
